message:
br-ju24y: format_g switches to %e when rounding overflows precision

C11 7.21.6.1 §8 classifies %g output using the pre-round exponent, but
glibc correctly re-classifies post-round: if the %f-style rounding
pushes the integer part past the precision digit budget (e.g. 999999.5
with precision 6 rounds to 1000000 = 7 digits), glibc switches to %e
style and emits "1e+06". Our format_g was committed to %f once chosen
and emitted "1000000", violating the precision contract.

Fix: after %f-style rendering, count the integer-part digits of the
result; if it exceeds precision, fall through to the %e branch.

Added regression test; 37/37 printf tests still pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Goal:

 {"id":"bd-5rs1","title":"syscall: fsmount invalid fsfd should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"On the current host, unprivileged fsmount(-1, 0, 0) and fsmount(-1, 1, 0) fail with EPERM rather than EBADF/EINVAL. Add focused ABI parity coverage so this privilege-first behavior stays explicit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:49:55.553436878Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:56:26.613206683Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:56:26.611053276Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add focused ABI regression coverage so the invalid-pidfd contract for this veneer is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:49:29.140778280Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:52:31.194324154Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:52:31.192191901Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity pidfd_send_signal(-1, 0, NULL, 0) behavior: exact EBADF and new ABI regression coverage; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-5t6zo","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] stdio printf family silently truncates return when total_len > INT_MAX","description":"crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs has 20 sites where snprintf/vsnprintf/sprintf/vsprintf and friends return total_len via 'as c_int' cast, truncating when the formatted-output length exceeds INT_MAX. POSIX requires EOVERFLOW + return -1 in that case (C11 7.21.6.5 p2 and POSIX.1-2024 snprintf). Concrete reach: a caller passing printf("%*d", INT_MAX, 42) or nested format specifiers that sum past 2^31 gets silent truncation of return value; downstream allocators that size buffers from the returned count are then under-sized and corruptable. Sites: lines 3284, 3354, 3404, 3484, 3499, 3549, 3627, 3642, 3674, 3727 (and 10 more). Fix: introduce printf_result_to_c_int(total_len) helper that returns -1 + sets errno to EOVERFLOW when total_len > c_int::MAX as usize, else total_len as c_int, and replace all 20 sites.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:40:45.030206230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:45:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:45:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Fixed in b00ce291: printf_result_to_c_int() helper returns -1 + EOVERFLOW when total_len overflows c_int; all 20 sites in snprintf/vsnprintf/sprintf/vsprintf family now use it. 102 stdio_abi integration tests still pass."}
+{"id":"bd-5t6zo","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] stdio printf family silently truncates return when total_len > INT_MAX","description":"crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs has 20 sites where snprintf/vsnprintf/sprintf/vsprintf and friends return total_len via 'as c_int' cast, truncating when the formatted-output length exceeds INT_MAX. POSIX requires EOVERFLOW + return -1 in that case (C11 7.21.6.5 p2 and POSIX.1-2024 snprintf). Concrete reach: a caller passing printf("%*d", INT_MAX, 42) or nested format specifiers that sum past 2^31 gets silent truncation of return value; downstream allocators that size buffers from the returned count are then under-sized and corruptable. Sites: lines 3284, 3354, 3404, 3484, 3499, 3549, 3627, 3642, 3674, 3727 (and 10 more). Fix: introduce printf_result_to_c_int(total_len) helper that returns -1 + sets errno to EOVERFLOW when total_len > c_int::MAX as usize, else total_len as c_int, and replace all 20 sites.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:40:45.030206230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:45:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed in b00ce291: printf_result_to_c_int() helper returns -1 + EOVERFLOW when total_len overflows c_int; all 20 sites in snprintf/vsnprintf/sprintf/vsprintf family now use it. 102 stdio_abi integration tests still pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-5vr","title":"Epic: Runtime Math Kernels - 70+ Controllers","description":"70+ runtime math modules: risk.rs (conformal bounds), bandit.rs (constrained MAB), control.rs (primal-dual), barrier.rs (SOS certificates), cohomology.rs (sheaf overlap), hji_reachability.rs (HJI safety), mean_field_game.rs (Lasry-Lions), schrodinger_bridge.rs (entropic OT), rough_path.rs (Lyons signatures), persistence.rs (persistent homology), large_deviations.rs (Cramer), eprocess.rs (anytime-valid), cvar.rs (tail-risk), alpha_investing.rs (FDR), spectral_monitor.rs (phase transitions), clifford.rs (SIMD), 50+ more. Branch-diversity constraint: >=3 families/milestone, no family >40%.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:32.607246126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:48:32.050030273Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:48:32.049815517Z","close_reason":"10/11 children closed. All runtime math kernels implemented (786 tests across 71 modules). Statistical, algebraic, topological, control, optimization kernels complete. Formal proofs complete. Reverse-round methodology complete. Only bd-10pq (aarch64 arch heterogeneity) remains as future work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["controllers","epic","frankenlibc","kernels","math"],"comments":[{"id":1032,"issue_id":"bd-5vr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:03Z"},{"id":1033,"issue_id":"bd-5vr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Card 1 dependencies acknowledged: runtime-math controller interactions must pass interference microbench and coverage drift guards.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:28:48Z"}]}

- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_kernel_framework -- --nocapture (PASS: 2 tests)","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:39:28Z"},{"id":1035,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (CloudyLynx): expanded runtime-kernel framework contract in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs.\n\nWhat I added:\n- New reverse-round branch-diversity contract types: RuntimeReverseRoundDiversityState + RuntimeReverseRoundDiversitySnapshot.\n- New RuntimeKernelFramework methods:\n  - reverse_round_diversity_snapshot()\n  - export_runtime_math_log_jsonl(mode, bead_id, run_id)\n- RuntimeMathKernel implementation for both methods; factored reverse-round diversity computation into reverse_round_diversity_snapshot_from_cards().\n- export_runtime_math_log_jsonl now consumes the explicit diversity snapshot contract for coverage/diversity event emission.\n- Added framework tests:\n  - runtime_kernel_framework_exposes_reverse_round_diversity_snapshot\n  - runtime_kernel_framework_exports_runtime_math_jsonl_logs\n\nValidation run (all via rch exec):\n- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets (PASS)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_kernel_framework -- --nocapture (PASS, 4 tests)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS)\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (PASS; existing warnings outside this change remain)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (FAIL due pre-existing unrelated warnings/errors in frankenlibc-core and frankenlibc-membrane tests, not introduced by this patch).\n\nLocal file-level formatting check:\n- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs (PASS)","created_at":"2026-02-25T07:05:36Z"},{"id":1036,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.1","author":"BoldFox","text":"Claimed by BoldFox.  {"id":"bd-5vr.2","title":"Math: Safety kernels - SOS barrier, HJI reachability, Galois connection","description":"Implement safety-critical kernels: sos_barrier.rs (O(d^2) quadratic form evaluation, <30ns), hji_reachability.rs (HJI differential game safety certificates), sos_invariant.rs (Lyapunov synthesis), barrier.rs (certificate evaluation). Certificates compiled as &'static const data with SHA-256 proof hashes.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

All integrated into RuntimeMathKernel. Validation: cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib => 894 passed, 0 failed.","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:38:25Z"}]}
@@ -707,6 +707,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-h5x.2","title":"Pthread primitive closure wave beyond mutex/condvar/thread bootstrap","description":"Background:
- Existing work addresses mutex/thread bootstrap, but remaining primitives can still hide glibc dependence.

Scope:
- Close remaining pthread primitive call-throughs (rwlock, barrier, once, attr/timeouts where in target scope).
- Ensure ABI paths route to internal/raw implementations with deterministic fallback policies.

Deliverables:
1) Primitive-by-primitive replacement plan.
2) Implemented routing updates and conformance fixtures.
3) Branch metrics for contention/wait/timeout behavior.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Target pthread primitives in replacement profile show zero disallowed call-through.
- Strict/hardened tests pass for covered primitives.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for state machines and edge/error paths.
- E2E concurrency fixtures with strict/hardened comparisons.
- Logs: trace_id, primitive, mode, path(internal/raw/fallback), errno, timing.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildCrane","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:26.799566701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:41:54.188485346Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:41:54.188197491Z","close_reason":"All pthread primitives have native implementations: mutex (normal/recursive/errorcheck), condvar (wait/signal/broadcast/timedwait/clockwait), rwlock, barrier, spinlock, once, TLS, cancel, naming, getattr_np, tryjoin_np, timedjoin_np, clockjoin_np. 142 exports, 169 tests. Remaining 5 resolve_host_symbol_raw calls are INTENTIONAL interpose fallbacks (host delegation for non-managed threads) — all have working native paths via FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","futex","pthread"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-c1x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-yos","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-z84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1099,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"WildCrane","text":"Progress slice landed: native cooperative cancellation path for pthread cancellation APIs in ABI layer.\n\nImplemented in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:\n- Replaced glibc call-through for pthread_cancel/pthread_setcancelstate/pthread_setcanceltype/pthread_testcancel.\n- Added thread-local cancellation state/type plus pending-request registry.\n- Added unit tests for API input validation and pending-cancel consume semantics.\n\nVerification attempted (all via rch):\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (fails due unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other files)\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (passed once, later blocked by unrelated membrane compile issue in runtime_math/evidence.rs: missing read_u32)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (fails due broad pre-existing lint debt outside this slice)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi pthread_cancel -- --nocapture (blocked by same unrelated membrane compile issue)\n\nCoordination:\n- Reserved pthread_abi + related conformance artifacts under reason bd-h5x.2.\n- support_matrix.json remains locked by SwiftLeopard, so matrix classification updates are pending coordination.","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:33:11Z"},{"id":1357,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"ChartreuseCrane","text":"ChartreuseCrane analysis: The 5 remaining pthread host delegations (pthread_getattr_np, pthread_cond_clockwait, pthread_timedjoin_np, pthread_tryjoin_np, pthread_clockjoin_np) are ALL intentional interpose delegations — they only fire for non-managed (glibc-created) objects. For FrankenLibC-managed objects, native futex/syscall paths already work. This is correct LD_PRELOAD behavior. These become dead code in replace mode. See bd-zh1y.1 for tracking.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:57:40Z"},{"id":1369,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"Progress update on pthread attr/cancel cleanup: patched pthread_mutex_init and pthread_cond_init host-delegating paths to reject destroyed attr words (word == 0) and invalid managed attr bitfields before translation. mutex_init_rejects_destroyed_attr, cond_init_rejects_destroyed_attr, and mutex_init_rejects_unsupported_extension_attributes now pass via rch. Follow-up fix in the same lane: converted the test-only FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING override from process-global behavior to a thread-local override with global fallback, then routed pthread ABI delegation checks through force_native_threading_enabled(). This removes cross-test interference in pthread_abi_test, where isolated passes were failing only in the full binary due to concurrent override leakage. Verification so far: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_check_lavendermarsh_pthreadfix cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests PASS, rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_ws_check_lavendermarsh6 cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS, rch exec -- cargo fmt --check PASS. Running remaining evidence now: full pthread_abi_test rerun and workspace clippy.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:56:33Z"},{"id":1370,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"Additional verification note: workspace clippy finished clean via rch (env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_ws_clippy_lavendermarsh6 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings).  {"id":"bd-h5x.3","title":"Non-threading call-through elimination + enforcement CI gate","description":"Background:
- Non-threading families (stdio/io/dlfcn/startup edges) can still conceal significant call-through debt.

Scope:
- Execute call-through elimination wave for high-traffic non-threading families prioritized by trace frequency and replacement-level necessity.
- Add CI gate that fails on forbidden call-through regressions.

Deliverables:
1) Ranked elimination backlog for non-threading families.
2) Replacement routing updates with fixture coverage.
3) Forbidden-call-through CI checker with clear triage output.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Forbidden call-through set trends to zero for declared level targets.
- Regression attempts fail CI with symbol-level diagnostics.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for checker policy logic.
- E2E smoke verification with replacement profile guard.
- Logs: trace_id, symbol, callthrough_detected, policy_rule, verdict, artifact_ref.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"Session 13 (RusticWolf): nativized 31 more dlsym_passthrough→Implemented in glibc_internal_abi.rs. Batches: printf-ext stubs (8), NSS stubs (7), pthread_cleanup TLS linked-list (4), __clone naked asm (1), misc stubs (3), obstack native (8). 69/69 integration tests pass. 31 dlsym_passthrough remain (all genuine GCT: resolver DNS 19, stdio vtable 6, dlfcn ext 4, f128 2). Matrix updated: Implemented=2042, GCT=350.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:26.919345712Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:16:06.943552178Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:16:06.943344906Z","close_reason":"Added race-free replacement-guard artifact paths, explicit policy verdict/log fields, and ranked non-threading backlog reporting with passing guard/test verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","dlfcn","stdio","syscall"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-24ug","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-27kh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-33zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1282,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","author":"Codex","text":"Codex progress (2026-03-10): nativized two remaining trivial stdio aliases in glibc_internal_abi by routing __fseeko64 -> crate::stdio_abi::fseeko64 and __ftello64 -> crate::stdio_abi::ftello64, eliminating host-glibc passthrough for both symbols. Added focused ABI integration coverage for valid tmpfile seek/tell behavior plus null-stream failure path, and updated support_matrix statuses for both symbols from GlibcCallThrough -> Implemented. Verification in this session: cargo fmt --check PASS; rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test PASS (155/155); rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS. Full workspace cargo test --all-targets exposed unrelated pre-existing failure in crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/process_abi_test.rs::waitid_with_p_pid (assert waitid should succeed, got -1).","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:11:43Z"},{"id":1283,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","author":"Codex","text":"Codex next slice (2026-03-10): continuing the non-threading callthrough burn-down under  by nativizing the  internal resolver-context functions in . ## Errno Routing Migration Complete

Commit 57b0825 converted all raw `*crate::errno_abi::__errno_location() = val` patterns to `crate::errno_abi::set_abi_errno(val)` across:
- glibc_internal_abi.rs (102 call sites)
- err_abi.rs (1 call site)
- unistd_abi.rs (1 call site, ftok null pathname guard)

The only remaining libc::__errno_location usage is inside set_abi_errno() itself (errno_abi.rs:53), which intentionally syncs our errno with host libc in interpose mode.

## Verification Commands
- Check no raw errno writes remain: grep returns 0
- set_abi_errno adoptions: 104 in glibc_internal_abi.rs
- errno_abi module tests: 2 passed
- Replacement guard census: call-throughs dropped from 289 to 235

## Delta
- Call-throughs: 289 → 235 (-54 symbols, 18.7% reduction)
- errno_abi now encapsulates all errno writes behind single function

+{"id":"bd-h7ede","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] printf float formatters panic on precision >= 65536 (process abort from any snprintf/fprintf/etc caller)","description":"Location: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs

Four float formatters feed user-controlled precision directly into Rust's format! machinery, which has an internal u16 limit on the precision field. Rust's formatter panics with 'Formatting argument out of range' when prec >= 65536, aborting the process.

Affected sites:
- format_f line 1284: alloc::format!("{:.prec$}", value, prec = precision)
- format_e line 1340-1344: alloc::format!("{:.prec$}{e_char}{sign}{abs_exp:02}", mantissa, prec = precision)
- format_g line 1370: alloc::format!("{:.prec$}", value, prec = frac_digits)
- format_a line 1421: core::iter::repeat_n('0', prec).collect::<String>() — allocates prec-sized string

Reproduction:
  let s = format!("{:.prec$}", 1.0f64, prec = 65536);
  // thread panicked at 'Formatting argument out of range'

Reachability: any C program calling snprintf/fprintf/printf/vsnprintf/etc with a format like '%.65536f' or '%.99999e' aborts the entire process. parse_decimal in the printf format parser uses saturating arithmetic so precision can reach usize::MAX; nothing between the parser and the float formatter caps it.

Severity: HIGH. DoS reachable from any user-controlled format string or adversarial library consumer. This is the exported libc.so surface.

Fix plan:
- Cap precision at 65535 at the top of format_float (for %f/%F/%e/%E/%g/%G) and inside format_a (for %a/%A). Rust's formatter accepts 65535 fine; anything above that already panics with today's code, so capping loses no currently-working behavior.
- Add regression unit tests covering the four float conversions at precision = 65536 and a round-trip check that precision = 65535 still works.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:46:45.987957564Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:46:48.973786862Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-jqy64","title":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: c11threads create/join backend mismatch","description":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: C11 thread create and join backend contract is internally inconsistent

Validated by current remote gate failure:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- failing suite: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs`

Concrete mismatch:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:72-110` calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_create`, which prefers host `pthread_create` whenever `resolved_thread_create_raw()` is available (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1991-2015`).
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:118-124` then calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_join`.
- `pthread_join` is hard-routed to `native_pthread_join` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2019-2021`), which only accepts handles present in `THREAD_HANDLE_REGISTRY` and returns `ESRCH` otherwise (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1409-1445`).

For host-created threads, the symbolic contract `join(create(f, arg)) = THRD_SUCCESS plus propagated return value` is broken: the handle identity model used by `create` is not the one consumed by `join`.

Observed fallout already matches that model break:
- `test_thrd_create_join`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_zero`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_negative`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_large`

Fix direction:
- make C11 thread lifecycle choose one backend consistently across create, join, detach, self, and equal, or
- teach `pthread_join` to resolve host-thread handles through the host path instead of assuming every successful create registered a native handle.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:20.777550950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:48:11.443759199Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","modes","review","symbolic"]}

Add isolated harness subprocess coverage for tests/conformance/fixtures/wide_string_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity per mode, and fix any divergence exposed by the matrix path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:12:06.305846872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T11:43:59.323959467Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T11:43:59.321768510Z","close_reason":"Added wide_string_ops_fixture_executes_with_host_parity_via_harness_matrix in commit be2558b — dispatches all 16 fixture cases through harness conformance-matrix-case subprocess (mirrors search_ops pattern). All cases pass via both in-process and subprocess paths. cargo check/clippy/test/fmt clean via rch + isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIR; ubs exit 0.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-ju24y","title":"REVIEW: [MEDIUM] printf %g does not switch to %e when rounding overflows precision","description":"crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs::format_g for value=999999.5 with default precision 6 renders '1000000' (7 digits), while glibc renders '1e+06'. The C11 7.21.6.1 para 8 rule: %g uses %e if exp<-4 OR exp>=precision. The pre-round exp check picks %f, but after rounding-half-to-even the integer part grows from 6 to 7 digits (999999.5 -> 1000000), which violates the precision contract; glibc re-classifies to %e in this case. Fix: after %f-style rendering, count the integer-part digits; if they exceed precision, re-render via format_e. Also confirmed divergence exists on same half-to-even rounding boundary (e.g. 99.95 with precision 3: glibc -> '100', ours would be '100' too — OK — but 999999.5 with default precision is the clean case).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:45:40.367348672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:45:40.367348672Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ju7","title":"Drift: runtime_math module cadence classification (hot path vs cadence; strict vs hardened)","description":"Create an explicit classification of every runtime_math module (existing + new) along these axes:

- Hot-path vs cadence:
  - `hot`: runs inside `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` strict fast path
  - `cadence`: runs only on epoch boundaries / resample points
- Mode surface:
  - used in strict
  - used only in hardened
- Data dependencies:
  - uses only integer/fixed-point state
  - uses floats (must be cadence-only if strict budget matters)
  - uses locks/mutex (must not be on strict hot path)

## Why This Matters
This is the “extreme optimization” guardrail: we can’t reason about perf without knowing which modules execute when.

## Deliverable
- A table (in a bead comment or short doc) listing each module and its classification.
- The table must include legacy anchors: which API families/subsystems the module protects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No module remains “unknown”; every module is classified.
- Any module marked `hot` has an explicit cost budget and no forbidden operations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.665023416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592931590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592907995Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1101,"issue_id":"bd-ju7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed by DustyPuma. Full cadence classification of all 45 runtime_math modules.

## Architecture Pattern: Deferred-Update

```
decide() [hot path, every call]
├─ AtomicU8/U64 reads (43 modules) — lock-free, integer only
├─ Direct method calls (2 modules: risk, cohomology) — lock-free
└─ Every 64th call: resample_high_order_kernels()
    └─ risk.evaluate() [lock-free], oracle.lock() [Mutex]

observe_validation_result() [cold path, after validation]
├─ 43 Mutex-protected modules updated sequentially
│  └─ Each state encoded to AtomicU8 and stored
└─ 25-element severity vector fed to fusion
   └─ 18 meta-controllers updated in second cascade
```

## Hot-Path Modules (2, both lock-free):
1. **risk** — `upper_bound_ppm()` direct call, conformal arithmetic, u32
2. **cohomology** — `fault_count()` direct call, u64 counter

## Cadence-Only Modules (43, behind Mutex in observe):
All follow the pattern: Mutex<Controller> updated in observe → state encoded to AtomicU8 → read lock-free in decide.

### AtomicU8 scalar (37 modules):
spectral, rough_path, persistence, bridge, hji, mfg, padic, symplectic, sparse, topos, audit, changepoint, conformal, loss_minimizer, coupling, microlocal, serre, clifford, ktheory, covering, tstructure, atiyah_bott, pomdp, sos, admm, obstruction, operator_norm, malliavin, info_geometry, matrix_concentration, nerve_complex, wasserstein, kernel_mmd, pac_bayes, stein, lyapunov, rademacher, transfer_entropy, hodge, provenance, grobner, grothendieck

### AtomicU8[COUNT] family-indexed (3 modules):
eprocess, cvar, large_deviations

### AtomicU64 multi-field (3 modules):
design (ident_ppm, budget_ns, expected_ns, selected), fusion (bonus_ppm, entropy_milli, drift_ppm, dominant_signal), equivariant (alignment_ppm, orbit)

## Mode Gating: NONE
All 45 modules run in BOTH strict and hardened modes. Mode only affects action selection (Allow vs Repair), not controller activation.

## Barrier Oracle:
Called lock-free in decide() at line 1253 — `barrier.admissible()`. Read-only guard, no observation needed. Classified as **hot/read-only**.

## Design Subsystem:
Special case — Mutex acquired in decide() but ONLY every 256th call (line ~1157). Cadence-gated.

## Summary:
- 0 modules with forbidden ops on strict hot path (no exp/ln/matrix)
- All float computation deferred to observe phase behind Mutex
- decide() hot path is 100% integer/atomic reads + 2 lock-free calls
- Strict overhead dominated by bonus summation (~40 atomic loads)","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:31Z"}]}
